sanskrit: यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं ततः । यस्मिन्स्थितो न दुःखेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: जिस लाभकी प्राप्ति होनेपर उससे अधिक कोई दूसरा लाभ उसके माननेमें भी नहीं आता और जिसमें स्थित होनेपर वह बड़े भारी दुःखसे भी विचलित नहीं किया जा सकता ॥
englishTranslation: Having attained it, one thinks no greater gain exists. Established in it, one is not shaken even by great sorrow.
essence: True fulfillment leaves nothing more to chase and nothing strong enough to unsettle you.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person who reaches this state sees nothing greater to attain and cannot be shaken even by severe suffering.
simpleInsight: There is calm in no longer needing life to be different.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna’s mind is being trained to settle beyond fear and restlessness. Krishna describes a state so complete that no other gain seems higher, and even heavy sorrow cannot disturb it.
modernRelevance: You get the message that would normally wreck your day, and your chest stays open anyway. Nothing inside you begs for a different reality.
translationLiteral: यम् (which) / लब्ध्वा (having attained) / च (and) / अपरम् (another) / लाभम् (gain) / मन्यते (one considers) / न (not) / अधिकम् (greater) / ततः (than that) / यस्मिन् (in which) / स्थितः (established) / न (not) / दुःखेन (by sorrow) / गुरुणा (heavy) / अपि (even) / विचाल्यते (is shaken)
